title: --net=host --privileged leaks /sys/fs/selinux
body:
Using `--net=host` *and* `--privileged` leaks in `/sys/fs/selinux`, which causes tools (e.g. `rpm`, `libvirt`) inside the container to think that SELinux is enabled, and try to perform domain transitions, which will cause failures as the filesystem types are `container_t`.

```
# rpm-ostree status -b
State: idle
AutomaticUpdates: ex-stage; rpm-ostreed-automatic.timer: last run 15h ago
Deployments:
● ostree://fedora-atomic:fedora/28/x86_64/workstation
                   Version: 28.20180911.0 (2018-09-11 15:06:50)
                BaseCommit: acfb4afdebf0731ce0b9134adf434173472a970328d75ece68d5015af27b33e4
# rpm -q docker podman
docker-1.13.1-61.git9cb56fd.fc28.x86_64
podman-0.8.4-2.git9f9b8cf.fc28.x86_64
# 
```

First, docker:

```
# docker run --privileged -ti --rm registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora:28 ls -al /sys/fs/selinux
total 0
dr-xr-xr-x. 2 root root 0 Sep 12 13:16 .
drwxr-xr-x. 9 root root 0 Sep 12 13:16 ..
host:root@vanadium tmp/fcos
# docker run --net=host --privileged -ti --rm registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora:28 ls -al /sys/fs/selinux
total 0
dr-xr-xr-x. 2 root root 0 Sep 11 20:55 .
drwxr-xr-x. 9 root root 0 Sep 11 20:55 ..
#
```

Now podman:

```
# podman run --privileged -ti --rm registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora:28 ls -al /sys/fs/selinux
total 0
dr-xr-xr-x. 2 root root 0 Sep 12 13:16 .
drwxr-xr-x. 9 root root 0 Sep 12 13:16 ..
# podman run --net=host --privileged -ti --rm registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora:28 ls -al /sys/fs/selinux
total 0
drwxr-xr-x.   7 root root    0 Sep 11 20:55 .
drwxr-xr-x.   9 root root    0 Sep 11 20:55 ..
-rw-rw-rw-.   1 root root    0 Sep 11 20:55 access
dr-xr-xr-x.   2 root root    0 Sep 11 20:55 avc
dr-xr-xr-x.   2 root root    0 Sep 11 20:55 booleans
-rw-r--r--.   1 root root    0 Sep 11 20:55 checkreqprot
dr-xr-xr-x. 131 root root    0 Sep 11 20:55 class
--w-------.   1 root root    0 Sep 11 20:55 commit_pending_bools
-rw-rw-rw-.   1 root root    0 Sep 11 20:55 context
-rw-rw-rw-.   1 root root    0 Sep 11 20:55 create
-r--r--r--.   1 root root    0 Sep 11 20:55 deny_unknown
--w-------.   1 root root    0 Sep 11 20:55 disable
-rw-r--r--.   1 root root    0 Sep 11 20:55 enforce
dr-xr-xr-x.   2 root root    0 Sep 11 20:55 initial_contexts
-rw-------.   1 root root    0 Sep 11 20:55 load
-rw-rw-rw-.   1 root root    0 Sep 11 20:55 member
-r--r--r--.   1 root root    0 Sep 11 20:55 mls
crw-rw-rw-.   1 root root 1, 3 Sep 11 20:55 null
-r--r--r--.   1 root root    0 Sep 11 20:55 policy
dr-xr-xr-x.   2 root root    0 Sep 11 20:55 policy_capabilities
-r--r--r--.   1 root root    0 Sep 11 20:55 policyvers
-r--r--r--.   1 root root    0 Sep 11 20:55 reject_unknown
-rw-rw-rw-.   1 root root    0 Sep 11 20:55 relabel
-r--r--r--.   1 root root    0 Sep 11 20:55 status
-rw-rw-rw-.   1 root root    0 Sep 11 20:55 user
--w--w--w-.   1 root root    0 Sep 11 20:55 validatetrans

comments.body:
i'm on rawhide atomic host `Rawhide.20180903.n.0` and I don't see this problem:

```
[root@vanilla-rawhide-atomic srv]# rpm -q podman
podman-0.8.5-1.dev.git65c31d4.fc30.x86_64
[root@vanilla-rawhide-atomic srv]# podman run --privileged -ti --rm registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora:28 ls -al /sys/fs/selinux
total 0
dr-xr-xr-x. 2 root root 0 Sep 12 13:45 .
drwxr-xr-x. 9 root root 0 Sep 12 13:45 ..
[root@vanilla-rawhide-atomic srv]# podman run --net=host --privileged -ti --rm registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora:28 ls -al /sys/fs/selinux
total 0
dr-xr-xr-x. 2 root root 0 Sep  5 13:41 .
drwxr-xr-x. 9 root root 0 Sep  5 13:41 ..

```

so maybe fixed already?
A --privileged container should be running as spc_t and we want libvirt to know about SELinux because we want it to launch its vms using svirt?
OK, so any tools that want to cause their children to think SELinux is disabled (`mock`, `rpm-ostree`, etc.) should unmount the filesystem?
